zotero.abstractNote: Learning stable dynamic systems from demonstrations is a fundamental problem in robot learning. While many approaches focus on deterministic systems, stochasticity is often required to represent the variability in human demonstrations. In this paper, we propose ImitationFlow, a novel framework for learning stable stochastic dynamic systems. We leverage normalizing flows to learn a transformation from a simple base distribution to a complex, multi-modal distribution of velocities. By enforcing a contraction constraint on the flow, we guarantee the stability of the learned system. We evaluate our approach on several synthetic and real-world robot manipulation tasks, demonstrating that ImitationFlow outperforms existing methods in terms of both accuracy and stability.
zotero.conferenceName: International Conference on Intelligent Robots and Systems (IROS)
zotero.proceedingsTitle: International Conference on Intelligent Robots and Systems (IROS)
